messages.content: You are a Lean 4/Mathlib context-planning agent. Prepare a compact context pack for formalizing a LaTeX theorem-like source unit. The target Lean declarations aligned to the selected source unit are withheld. Return exactly one JSON object.

{
  "rules": [
    "Do not infer or reveal hidden target Lean declaration names for the selected unit.",
    "Do not write theorem/lemma Lean code in target_statement_sketch; exact API syntax belongs in needed_mathlib_context and will be hydrated by tools.",
    "Do not bundle all source parts into one conjunction unless the source unit itself requires that shape.",
    "Use previous project declarations only if they are shown under prior_project_context.",
    "Use local_file_predecessor_declarations only as same-file helper/style context; the selected unit's aligned/referencing target declarations are omitted.",
    "Do not treat Mathlib as the only context; enumerate source/project/local/Mathlib context separately.",
    "Prefer exact Mathlib names when known; otherwise give a narrow query plus expected signature shape.",
    "Keep added context tight: prefer a few thousand tokens or less per source unit."
  ],
  "schema": {
    "units": [
      {
        "context_pack_size_risk": "low|medium|high",
        "formalization_risks": [
          "source ambiguity, missing notation, broad multi-part theorem"
        ],

            "missing_or_uncertain_context": [

                "expected_signature_or_shape": "expected type/signature/docstring",
                "name_or_query": "exact Mathlib name or narrow search query",
                "why_needed": "definition/proof/tactic support"
              }
            ],
            "needed_project_context": [
              {
                "name": "previous project declaration if provided or likely needed",
                "why_needed": "supporting theorem/definition/notation"
              }
            ],
            "needed_source_context": [
              "source labels/statements"
            ],
            "source_part": "whole unit or part marker",
            "target_statement_sketch": "prose mathematical target sketch; do not write exact Lean syntax, declaration names, or guessed API argument order",
            "task_id": "unit-001-task-1"
          }
        ],
        "selector_confidence": 0.0,
        "source_focus_summary": "short summary",
        "unit_key": "unit-001"
      }
    ]
  },
  "task": "For each LaTeX source unit, decompose the source into ordered Lean declaration tasks and select tight context for each task. The context inventory must be separate: source text, previous book/source statements, previous project declarations, local file/import/style context, and selected Mathlib APIs.",

          "lean_snippet": "def size (μ : NPartition N) : ℕ := ∑ i, μ.parts i",

          "lean_snippet": "theorem ext {μ ν : NPartition N} (h : μ.parts = ν.parts) : μ = ν := by\n  cases μ; cases ν; simp_all",

          "lean_snippet": "theorem ext' {μ ν : NPartition N} : μ = ν ↔ ∀ i, μ.parts i = ν.parts i :=\n  ⟨fun h _ => h ▸ rfl, fun h => ext (funext h)⟩",

          "lean_snippet": "theorem part_eq_parts (μ : NPartition N) (i : Fin N) : μ.part i = μ.parts i := rfl",

        {
          "benchmark_honesty": "Uses post-hoc target file/line placement only to select earlier declarations; current-unit aligned/referencing declarations are omitted.",
          "context_source": "same_file_before_selected_unit_line",
          "kind": "lemma",
          "lean_snippet": "private lemma filter_ne_zero_sum (m : Multiset ℕ) : (m.filter (· ≠ 0)).sum = m.sum := by\n  induction m using Multiset.induction_on with\n  | empty => simp\n  | cons a s ih =>\n    simp only [Multiset.filter_cons, Multiset.sum_cons]\n    split_ifs with ha\n    · simp [ih]\n    · push_neg at ha; simp [ha, ih]",
          "line_range": [
            529,
            542
          ],
          "name": "filter_ne_zero_sum",
          "path": "AlgebraicCombinatorics/SymmetricFunctions/NPartition.lean",
          "snippet_truncated": false
        },
        {
          "benchmark_honesty": "Uses post-hoc target file/line placement only to select earlier declarations; current-unit aligned/referencing declarations are omitted.",
          "context_source": "same_file_before_selected_unit_line",
          "kind": "def",
          "lean_snippet": "def toPartition (μ : NPartition N) : Nat.Partition μ.size where\n  parts := (Finset.univ.val.map μ.parts).filter (· ≠ 0)\n  parts_pos := by\n    intro i hi\n    simp only [Multiset.mem_filter, Multiset.mem_map, Finset.mem_val, Finset.mem_univ,\n      true_and] at hi\n    omega\n  parts_sum := by\n    simp only [size]\n    have h : (Finset.univ.val.map μ.parts).sum = ∑ i, μ.parts i := by\n      simp [Finset.sum_eq_multiset_sum]\n    rw [← h]\n    exact filter_ne_zero_sum _",
          "line_range": [
            563,
            576
          ],

          "path": "AlgebraicCombinatorics/SymmetricFunctions/NPartition.lean",
          "snippet_truncated": false

          "reason": "Earlier or explicitly referenced source theorem-like unit.",
          "source_unit": {
            "environment": "definition",

            "labels": [

            ],
            "parse_warnings": [],
            "part_markers": [],
            "path": "AlgebraicCombinatorics/tex/SymmetricFunctions/MonomialSymmetric.tex",
            "referenced_labels": [],
            "source_text": "\\begin{definition}\n\\label{def.sf.Npar}An $N$\\emph{-partition} will mean a weakly decreasing\n$N$-tuple of nonnegative integers. In other words, an $N$-partition means an\n$N$-tuple $\\left(  \\lambda_{1},\\lambda_{2},\\ldots,\\lambda_{N}\\right)\n\\in\\mathbb{N}^{N}$ with $\\lambda_{1}\\geq\\lambda_{2}\\geq\\cdots\\geq\\lambda_{N}$.\n\\end{definition}"
          }
        }
      ],
      "prior_project_context": [
        {
          "project_declarations": [
            {
              "context_source": "referencing_prior_declaration",
              "declared_source_labels": [],
              "imports": [
                "Mathlib",
                "AlgebraicCombinatorics.SymmetricFunctions.NPartition"
              ],
              "kind": "abbrev",
              "lean_snippet": "/-- Alias for the canonical `NPartition` type within this namespace.\n    An N-partition is a weakly decreasing N-tuple of nonnegative integers.\n    (Definition def.sf.Npar)\n\n    This is represented as a function `Fin N → ℕ` that is antitone\n    (i.e., `i ≤ j → parts j ≤ parts i`).\n\n    All basic operations (`size`, `length`, `zero`, etc.) and lemmas are inherited\n    from the canonical `_root_.NPartition` definition in `NPartition.lean`. -/\nabbrev NPartition (N : ℕ) := _root_.NPartition N\n\nnamespace NPartition\n\n-- All basic definitions and lemmas (ext, DecidableEq, size, zero, length, etc.)\n-- are inherited from _root_.NPartition in NPartition.lean.\n\n/-!\n## Bijection with partitions of length ≤ N (Proposition prop.sf.Npar-as-par)\n\nThere is a bijection between partitions of length ≤ N and N-partitions.\n\nThe core definitions (`ofPartition`, `toPartition`, `ofPartition_size`, `toPartition_card_le`)\nare defined in `NPartition.lean` and re-exported here for convenience within this namespace.\n-/\n\n-- Re-export canonical definitions from _root_.NPartition to this namespace.\n-- This allows code in this file to use `ofPartition`, `toPartition`, etc.\n-- without qualification, while avoiding code duplication.",
              "line_range": [
                91,
                118
              ],
              "module": "AlgebraicCombinatorics.SymmetricFunctions.MonomialSymmetric",
              "name": "AlgebraicCombinatorics.SymmetricFunctions.NPartition",
              "path": "AlgebraicCombinatorics/SymmetricFunctions/MonomialSymmetric.lean",
              "referenced_source_labels": [
                "def.sf.Npar"
              ],
              "snippet_policy": "statement_or_definition_body_only",
              "snippet_truncated": false
            },
            {
              "context_source": "referencing_prior_declaration",
              "declared_source_labels": [],
              "imports": [
                "Mathlib"
              ],
              "kind": "structure",
              "lean_snippet": "/-- An N-partition is a weakly decreasing N-tuple of nonnegative integers.\n    (Definition def.sf.Npar)\n\n    This is represented as a function `Fin N → ℕ` that is antitone\n    (i.e., `i ≤ j → parts j ≤ parts i`).\n\n    The field is named `antitone` to match Mathlib conventions. -/\nstructure NPartition (N : ℕ) where\n  /-- The entries of the N-partition as a function from `Fin N` to `ℕ` -/\n  parts : Fin N → ℕ\n  /-- The entries are weakly decreasing (antitone) -/\n  antitone : Antitone parts\n\nnamespace NPartition\n\nvariable {N : ℕ}\n\n/-! ## Compatibility aliases for field names -/",
              "line_range": [
                125,
                142
              ],
              "module": "AlgebraicCombinatorics.SymmetricFunctions.NPartition",
              "name": "NPartition",
              "path": "AlgebraicCombinatorics/SymmetricFunctions/NPartition.lean",
              "referenced_source_labels": [
                "def.sf.Npar"
              ],
              "snippet_policy": "statement_or_definition_body_only",
              "snippet_truncated": false
            }

      },
      "source_unit": {
        "environment": "proposition",
        "id": "AlgebraicCombinatorics/tex/SymmetricFunctions/MonomialSymmetric.tex:prop.sf.Npar-as-par",
        "labels": [

        ],
        "line_range": [

          31
        ],
        "parse_warnings": [],
        "part_markers": [],
        "path": "AlgebraicCombinatorics/tex/SymmetricFunctions/MonomialSymmetric.tex",
        "referenced_labels": [],
        "source_text": "\\begin{proposition}\n\\label{prop.sf.Npar-as-par}There is a bijection%\n\\begin{align*}\n\\left\\{  \\text{partitions of length }\\leq N\\right\\}   &  \\rightarrow\\left\\{\nN\\text{-partitions}\\right\\}  ,\\\\\n\\left(  \\lambda_{1},\\lambda_{2},\\ldots,\\lambda_{\\ell}\\right)   &\n\\mapsto\\left(  \\lambda_{1},\\lambda_{2},\\ldots,\\lambda_{\\ell}%\n,\\underbrace{0,0,\\ldots,0}_{N-\\ell\\text{ zeroes}}\\right)  .\n\\end{align*}\n\n\\end{proposition}"
      },
